title: Bridge Essay: Cerebellar circuitry and auditory verbal hallucinations: An integrative synthesis and perspective.
concept: Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Cerebellar circuitry and auditory verbal hallucinations: An integrative synthesis and perspective.** - Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2020.08.004. PMID: 32810512. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) - experienced as voice hearing independent of a corresponding external sound source - are a cardinal symptom of psychosis. Approximately 6-13% of healthy individuals also experience voice hearing. Despite numerous attempts to explain the neurofunctional mechanisms underlying AVH, they remain notoriously unexplained. However, evidence relates AVH to mechanistic changes in the forward model. This review synthesizes behavioral and neuroimaging studies exploring
